text.source_text: पूर्वं जाठराग्निप्रतीको जाठराग्न्युपाधिको वा परमेश्वर उपास्य इत्युक्तम् अन्तःप्रतिष्ठितत्वाद्यनुरोधेन । इदानीं तु विनैव प्रतीकोपाधिकल्पनाभ्यां साक्षादपि परमेश्वरोपासनपरिग्रहे न कश्चिद्विरोध इति जैमिनिराचार्यो मन्यते । ननु जाठराग्न्यपरिग्रहेऽन्तःप्रतिष्ठितत्ववचनं शब्दादीनि च कारणानि विरुध्येरन्निति । अत्रोच्यते — अन्तःप्रतिष्ठितत्ववचनं तावन्न विरुध्यते । न हीह ‘ पुरुषविधं पुरुषेऽन्तः प्रतिष्ठितं वेद ’ इति जाठराग्न्यभिप्रायेणेदमुच्यते , तस्याप्रकृतत्वादसंशब्दितत्वाच्च । कथं तर्हि ? यत्प्रकृतं मूर्धादिचुबुकान्तेषु पुरुषावयवेषु पुरुषविधत्वं कल्पितम् , तदभिप्रायेणेदमुच्यते — ‘ पुरुषविधं पुरुषेऽन्तः प्रतिष्ठितं वेद ’ इति । यथा वृक्षे शाखां प्रतिष्ठितां पश्यतीति , तद्वत् । अथवा यः प्रकृतः परमात्मा अध्यात्ममधिदैवतं च पुरुषविधत्वोपाधिः , तस्य यत्केवलं साक्षिरूपम् , तदभिप्रायेणेदमुच्यते — ‘ पुरुषविधं पुरुषेऽन्तः प्रतिष्ठितं वेद ’ इति । निश्चिते च पूर्वापरालोचनवशेन परमात्मपरिग्रहे , तद्विषय एव वैश्वानरशब्दः केनचिद्योगेन वर्तिष्यते — विश्वश्चायं नरश्चेति , विश्वेषां वायं नरः , विश्वे वा नरा अस्येति विश्वानरः परमात्मा , सर्वात्मत्वात् , विश्वानर एव वैश्वानरः । तद्धितोऽनन्यार्थः , राक्षसवायसादिवत् । अग्निशब्दोऽप्यग्रणीत्वादियोगाश्रयणेन परमात्मविषय एव भविष्यति । गार्हप त्या दिकल्पनं प्राणाहुत्यधिकरणत्वं च परमात्मनोऽपि सर्वात्मत्वादुपपद्यते ॥ २८ ॥
text.transliteration_iast: pūrvaṃ jāṭharāgnipratīko jāṭharāgnyupādhiko vā parameśvara upāsya ityuktam antaḥpratiṣṭhitatvādyanurodhena | idānīṃ tu vinaiva pratīkopādhikalpanābhyāṃ sākṣādapi parameśvaropāsanaparigrahe na kaścidvirodha iti jaiminirācāryo manyate | nanu jāṭharāgnyaparigrahe'ntaḥpratiṣṭhitatvavacanaṃ śabdādīni ca kāraṇāni virudhyeranniti | atrocyate — antaḥpratiṣṭhitatvavacanaṃ tāvanna virudhyate | na hīha ‘ puruṣavidhaṃ puruṣe'ntaḥ pratiṣṭhitaṃ veda ’ iti jāṭharāgnyabhiprāyeṇedamucyate , tasyāprakṛtatvādasaṃśabditatvācca | kathaṃ tarhi ? yatprakṛtaṃ mūrdhādicubukānteṣu puruṣāvayaveṣu puruṣavidhatvaṃ kalpitam , tadabhiprāyeṇedamucyate — ‘ puruṣavidhaṃ puruṣe'ntaḥ pratiṣṭhitaṃ veda ’ iti | yathā vṛkṣe śākhāṃ pratiṣṭhitāṃ paśyatīti , tadvat | athavā yaḥ prakṛtaḥ paramātmā adhyātmamadhidaivataṃ ca puruṣavidhatvopādhiḥ , tasya yatkevalaṃ sākṣirūpam , tadabhiprāyeṇedamucyate — ‘ puruṣavidhaṃ puruṣe'ntaḥ pratiṣṭhitaṃ veda ’ iti | niścite ca pūrvāparālocanavaśena paramātmaparigrahe , tadviṣaya eva vaiśvānaraśabdaḥ kenacidyogena vartiṣyate — viśvaścāyaṃ naraśceti , viśveṣāṃ vāyaṃ naraḥ , viśve vā narā asyeti viśvānaraḥ paramātmā , sarvātmatvāt , viśvānara eva vaiśvānaraḥ | taddhito'nanyārthaḥ , rākṣasavāyasādivat | agniśabdo'pyagraṇītvādiyogāśrayaṇena paramātmaviṣaya eva bhaviṣyati | gārhapa tyā dikalpanaṃ prāṇāhutyadhikaraṇatvaṃ ca paramātmano'pi sarvātmatvādupapadyate || 28 ||

translation.literal_translation: Previously it was said that what is to be worshiped is either the Supreme Lord symbolized by digestive fire, or the Supreme Lord qualified by digestive-fire adjunct, in deference to statements like inner-establishment. But now Acarya Jaimini holds that even without imagining symbol or adjunct there is no contradiction in taking direct worship of the Supreme Lord. Objection: if digestive fire is not taken, then statements of inner-establishment and verbal indicators would conflict. Reply: the inner-establishment statement does not conflict. Here 'he knows the person-formed one established within the person' is not said with digestive fire in mind, since that is neither the current topic nor directly named. How then? It is said with reference to the person-form previously projected in bodily limbs from head down to chin, just as one says a branch is established in a tree. Or it is said with reference to the pure witness-aspect of that Supreme Self who, as previously discussed, bears both adhyatma and adhidaivata person-form adjuncts. Once examination of prior and later context settles that the intended referent is the Supreme Self, the term Vaishvanara can be connected to Him through derivation: this is the universal person; or all persons are His; thus Visvanara, and Vaishvanara by taddhita without change of meaning. Even 'agni' can apply to the Supreme through derivational sense such as leader/guide. Constructions like Garhapatya etc. and being locus of prana-offerings are also fitting for the Supreme because of His all-selfhood. (28)
translation.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.2.28 presents Jaimini's saksat-reading: Vaisvanara-upasana may target Brahman directly; lexical and ritual markers are reinterpreted via contextual primacy and etymological-yogic extension.
translation.interpretive_note: Moves from mediated symbol/upadhi reading to direct Brahman-reference without abandoning textual indicators.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Jaimini allows direct Brahman-reference of Vaisvanara without mandatory jatharagni-symbol mediation.
Earlier interpretive option acknowledged, but no longer required as exclusive reading.
Affirms avirodha in taking Vaisvanara as Brahman directly in this context.
Semantic conflict is resolved by contextual-linga priority over routine fire-term usage.
Used to broaden Vaisvanara beyond narrow jatharagni literalism.

uncertainty_notes: Curated in contiguous S02 pass with stable cadence and inferential-tag schema.
A later philological pass should revisit pradesa-matra, sampatti, and recensional lexical correspondences in records 1.2.071-1.2.078.
